page: Microsoft Azure DC8ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCADCCV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance provides 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and includes a 307 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability. No GPUs are bundled with this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate a strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the top 25% at 2692.28. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth present distinct tradeoffs. While small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is average at 514.34 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidths fall into the bottom 25% at 38.31 GB/sec and 22.92 GB/sec, respectively. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is also weak, scoring 35.48 GB/sec, whereas single-threaded operations like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression maintain average performance tiers.

This server configuration is suitable for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory resources with moderate local storage needs, such as static web serving, database operations, and software compilation. The presence of dedicated AMD EPYC cores offers predictable execution for single-threaded tasks, though memory-intensive applications processing large datasets may experience bottlenecks due to the lower uncached memory bandwidth. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides a balanced resource density of 4 GB RAM per vCPU alongside dedicated CPU allocation, making it a viable option for general-purpose enterprise applications and moderate database hosting.
description: Microsoft Azure DC8ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DCADCCV5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz, delivering 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 307 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, though multi-core performance and uncached large-block memory bandwidth are weaker. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that do not require GPU acceleration.

Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 307 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability
